steps.title: Use exponential properties
steps.content: Given $ f(x) = c^x $ and $ f(7) = 16 \cdot f(5) $, substitute into the equation: $$ c^7 = 16 \cdot c^5 $$ Divide both sides by $ c^5 $: $$ c^2 = 16 $$

steps.title: Solve for c
steps.content: Since $ c \gt 1 $, take the positive square root: $$ c = \sqrt{16} = 4 $$
